text:
P2-15. Harmonize the pain headline: README "The problem is the wire, and the wire is you" vs landing "The problem is the wire. The wire is you." The landing's two-sentence version is punchier — adopt in README.
 [...] - **HTTPS clone URLs** (both were SSH — broke copy-paste for anyone without GitHub keys).
- Ported the cleaner landing lines back ("You're owed the boundary", "customs office"); table cell → "central registry"; harmonized the drifted pain-headline and metaphor.
 [...] 
P2-12. Landing h2: "The hooks are the straw. The fabric is the milkshake." The skill endorses analogies, and in the README the metaphor lands because the explaining bullet is adjacent. As a cold section heading it's a riddle before the referent. Demote it to the first body line; promote something plain to h2 — e.g. "Hosts adapt to it — not the other way around."
 [...] - Killed the false-ringing **"Nothing leaves your keys"** → *"Your keys never leave your disk."*
- Added trust clause to the `@mention` card; active voice on distill; demoted the "straw/milkshake" riddle out of a cold heading.
- **Added a "Common questions" strip** before the final CTA (the biggest missing conversion element per the skill) — who-can-message / whose-LLM / relay-dies / the demo risk-reversal.
